Type of Agent in Treatment Arm.value: Immunotherapeutic (anti-PD-1 monoclonal antibody) and Antiangiogenic (VEGF receptor tyrosine kinase inhibitor)
Type of Agent in Treatment Arm.reasoning: Page 2, in the 'BACKGROUND' section, states, 'Both the VEGF receptor tyrosine kinase inhibitor axitinib and the anti-programmed death 1 (PD-1) monoclonal antibody pembrolizumab have shown antitumor activity'. This identifies pembrolizumab as an anti-PD-1 monoclonal antibody (immunotherapeutic) and axitinib as a VEGF receptor tyrosine kinase inhibitor (antiangiogenic).

Risk Category Criteria.value: International Metastatic Renal Cell Carcinoma Database Consortium (IMDC) risk group criteria: Karnofsky performance-status score of less than 80, time from initial diagnosis to randomization of less than 1 year, hemoglobin level below the lower limit of the normal range, corrected serum calcium level above the upper limit of the normal range, absolute neutrophil count above the upper limit of the normal range, and platelet count above the upper limit of the normal range. Favorable risk = IMDC score of 0; Intermediate risk = IMDC score of 1 or 2; Poor risk = IMDC score of 3 to 6.
Risk Category Criteria.reasoning: Found in the "TRIAL DESIGN AND TREATMENTS" section on page 2 and Table 1 footnote † on page 4, which describe the IMDC risk score and its six risk factors.
